scraped_bill_text.title: Short-Lived Climate Pollutants: Methane Emissions: Dairy and Livestock: Organic Waste: Landfills
scraped_bill_text.text: An act to add Sections 39730, 39730.5, and 42652 to, and to add and repeal Section 42652.5 of, the Health and Safety Code, and to add Sections 42652, 42652.5, 42653, 42654, and 42655 to, the Public Resources Code, relating to greenhouse gases. SEC. 2. Section 39730 is added to the Health and Safety Code, to read: 39730. (a) The state board shall approve and begin implementing a comprehensive strategy to reduce emissions of short-lived climate pollutants. (b) The strategy shall achieve a reduction in methane by 40 percent, hydrofluorocarbon gases by 40 percent, and anthropogenic black carbon by 50 percent below 2013 levels by 2030. (c) The state board shall adopt regulations to reduce methane emissions from livestock manure management operations by up to 40 percent below the dairy and livestock sector's 2013 methane emissions levels by January 1, 2030.

rag_chunks.content: SB 1383 requires 40 percent reduction in methane emissions from livestock manure management operations by 2030, and 50 percent reduction in anthropogenic black carbon.

web_sources.title: CARB Short-Lived Climate Pollutants Program
web_sources.snippet: SB 1383 establishes emissions reduction targets for methane, hydrofluorocarbons, and black carbon. Implementation costs estimated at $100-300 million annually.
